msgs.text:
Turn 32 — the first t=s−1 coefficient layer is contractible I re-read only the three attachments for this turn. The standing problem is still the over-G isomorphism question for ΓS,ΓH, with F=P⋊G, P the free G-operator pro-2 group on x0,x1, and the required condition πH∘Φ=πS.

The new result is:the first t=s−1 coefficient layer R/(2,t2) gives no obstruction.This clears the separate t-derivation check left open at the end of Turn 31. The remaining coefficient obstruction is now concentrated in the 2-Bockstein secondary tower, starting with the already-defined mapsBd:kerDd+1,…,2d−1⟶Hd(2).

Turn 31 killed the first A2=R/(4,t) secondary scalar H4(2), but left H5(2),H6(2),… and the separate R/(2,t2) derivation direction open.Set

R:=EfΛ≃Z2 [[t]],t=s−1.

After the H1 -change from Turn 1, the dangerous Ef-row is(s2+1, s−1)=(2+2t+t2, t).

Thus the two first coefficient quotients split as

R/(4,t):(s2+1,s−1)=(2,0),and

R/(2,t2):(s2+1,s−1)=(0,t).The history had already identified this split: R/(4,t) is the 2-Witt/Bockstein direction, while R/(2,t2) is a derivational t-direction.

Now work overAt:=R/(2,t2)≅F2 [t]/(t2).

Here s=1+t, s2=1, and t2=0. Therefore all t-coefficient corrections are genuinely linear: if V∈Ln is primitive, then1+tV is group-like to all relevant finite Magnus orders, because the quadratic term t2V2 vanishes.At a finite Magnus stage, suppose the first t-coefficient error is1+tΩn+O(In+1),Ωn∈Ln.The row (0,t) means that a correction in the second Nielsen coordinate, equivalently the Y1 -coordinate after the transported H1 -basis, changes the leading t-tail byΩn⟼Ωn+V.

Choosing V=Ωn kills the first t-tail. Since t2=0, this correction creates no restricted-square secondary term V[2]at the same coefficient level. Any higher t-tail produced by the ordinary commutator part is again linear and primitive, so the same step repeats.

Equivalently, the At -linearized correction complex has a contracting section supplied by the second coordinate:Ω⟼(0,Ω,0).So the first t-coefficient layer is contractible. This is stronger than the earlier observation that the Y0↦Y0Y4 lower seed was not blocked by the t-ghost at the degree-16 bottleneck; it says that the entire first t-derivation layer R/(2,t2) is harmless, degree by degree.This also clarifies why the t-case should not be merged with the A2=R/(4,t) case. In the 2-direction, an actual square correction has the coupled ghostK2=1+2W+W[2]+⋯,so the secondary quotient Hd(2)is real. In the first t-direction, the corresponding square term is killed by t2=0, so there is no t-analogue of Hd(2)at this layer.This does not prove the full finite coefficient nonemptiness lemma. Higher coefficient layers such as t2, 2t, and 4 can again interact with restricted-square terms, because a correction with coefficient t has square coefficient t2, and a correction with coefficient 2 has square coefficient 4. So the correct global coefficient induction still has to handle iterated m=(2,t)-adic ghosts.But Turn 32 removes one independent branch of the search tree:

R/(2,t2)is not a source of a first coefficient obstruction.

Thus the immediate remaining obstruction is exactly the A2=R/(4,t) secondary tower isolated in Turns 30–31:Hd(2)=Ld[2]/imθd0.

Turn 30 computeddimHd(2)=1,3,7,12,21,43,81(d=4,…,10),and Turn 31 killed the first scalar H4(2)using the Bockstein image of the degree 5,6,7 continuation kernel.So the next useful computation is now cleaner: compute the actual A2 -secondary defect inH5(2)and test it modulo the image ofB5:kerD6,7,8,9⟶H5(2).

If the H5(2)-defect dies, continue with B6,B7,B8. If the maps Bd contain the actual defects uniformly, then the coefficient proof should be promoted to an m-adic Bockstein induction. If one defect survives, it becomes the first serious finite-coefficient obstruction candidate.

ESTABLISHED: The first t=s−1 coefficient layer R/(2,t2) is contractible. Over this quotient the dangerous row is (0,t), and t2=0 removes restricted-square secondary terms, so every first t-coefficient primitive tail is killed linearly by a second-coordinate correction. Together with Turn 31, the first t-layer and the first A2 -secondary scalar H4(2)are both cleared.

GAP: The full finite coefficient nonemptiness lemma remains unproved. The A2=R/(4,t) secondary obstruction spaces H5(2),H6(2),… still have to be tested against the continuation-Bockstein maps Bd. Higher m-adic layers such as t2,2t,4 are also not yet covered.

NEXT: ComputeB5:kerD6,7,8,9→H5(2)and the actual H5(2)-defect. Do not test a fixed H5 -tail alone; quotient it by the full continuation-Bockstein image, exactly as Turn 31 did for μ4.[TURN32-FIRST-T-DERIVATION-LAYER-CONTRACTIBLE]
